evals.prompt: I have a React + Vite task management app running at localhost:5173. It has routes for / (home), /tasks, /tasks/new, /tasks/:id, and /profile. Users can create, edit, delete, and complete tasks. Run adversarial tests on it.
evals.expected_output: Detects React + Vite stack, generates Playwright test files for happy path flows (CRUD tasks), CSS visual audit at 3 viewports, and adversarial attacks (double-submit, input boundary, XSS probes, rapid toggle, etc.). Produces a structured markdown report with screenshots and severity classifications.
expectations: Detects the stack as React with Vite
Writes Playwright test files to the project
Tests happy path flows for task CRUD operations
Runs adversarial attacks including XSS/SQL injection probes
Produces a structured markdown report with severity classifications

evals.prompt: My SvelteKit e-commerce site at localhost:5173 has some weird layout issues on mobile. The checkout flow at /checkout has a multi-step form. Can you check the CSS and try to break the checkout?
evals.expected_output: Detects SvelteKit stack, runs CSS audit at mobile/tablet/desktop viewports with focus on mobile layout issues, tests the checkout flow as a happy path, runs adversarial attacks targeting the multi-step form (mid-action navigation, session expiry, input boundary). Produces report focused on mobile visual issues and checkout vulnerabilities.
expectations: Runs CSS visual audit at 375px, 768px, 1280px viewports
Tests the checkout flow as a happy path
Runs adversarial attacks targeting the multi-step checkout form

evals.prompt: I just finished an Expo React Native app for tracking habits. It's running on iOS simulator. Run adversarial QA on it — I'm especially worried about the habit streak counter and the settings screen.
evals.expected_output: Detects Expo/React Native native app, reads native-testing.md reference, sets up Detox, runs mobile-specific adversarial attacks (device rotation, background/foreground, touch stress) with focus on the habit streak counter and settings screen. Produces a report with mobile-specific severity classifications.
expectations: Detects the stack as React Native / Expo
Tests mobile-specific adversarial attacks like device rotation and background/foreground
Focuses testing on habit streak counter and settings screen
Produces a structured markdown report with native-specific findings
